title: Active UWB experiments: Experiments hub + Scanner/Beacon/Transponder/Fuzzer
description:
Turn the receive-only sniffer into an active-TX explorer. Four experiments surfaced in a single 'Experiments' hub on BOTH app surfaces (iOS app + Pi web dashboard).

Architecture facts established during scoping:
- Two firmware personalities: (a) STOCK CLI (cli.hex) driven Pi-side over serial via uwb_explorer/device.py — already sends initf/respf/uwbcfg/listener/stop; (b) CUSTOM board-only BLE firmware (firmware/ble/) with an existing control-command downlink (F1/F0/S<sts>).
- Control downlink ALREADY EXISTS end-to-end: iOS BLEManager.writeValue() writes short string commands to the 6e5f control characteristic (BLEManager.swift ~L256-262); both firmware/ble/ble_app.c and Pi uwb_explorer/ble.py implement the 6e5f000x service. Experiments EXTEND this opcode family, not invent a new path.
- Half-duplex: the board can sniff OR transmit, not both — an experiment pauses the sniffer loop.
- Scanner(#1) & Transponder(#4) need NO new firmware (stock INITF/RESPF via Device). Beacon(#2) works as a fixed-frame TCFM beacon on stock fw; arbitrary payload needs custom fw. Fuzzer(#3) needs custom-fw malformed-frame TX.
- HELD OFF (not in this epic): AirTag replay + CW jamming (ideas #5/#6) pending an OK-to-do review.

TDD: user requires red/green TDD. Python controllers are TDD-able with tests/test_device.py ScriptedSerial + tests/test_web.py FakeDev (no hardware). Firmware frame-BUILDERS are TDD-able in tests/c/ (see test_framefmt/test_framepoll); only the actual radio TX needs on-board verification.

Suggested build order: Foundation (4) -> Scanner -> Transponder -> Beacon -> Fuzzer. Firmware beads (beacon-payload, fuzzer-tx) are the only ones needing the board in the loop.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed; Experiments hub live on web + iOS; scanner & transponder work on stock fw; beacon + fuzzer firmware verified on-board; docs/EXPERIMENTS.md documents the control opcodes; full test suite green.

title: Device: add active-TX CLI helpers (initf/respf/uwbcfg/tcfm)
description: Extend uwb_explorer/device.py Device with thin helpers the experiments build on, mirroring existing send()/_command_blocks()/_UWBCFG_ORDER: set_uwbcfg(**params) (rewrite preserving unset fields, like set_channel), start_initf(**params), start_respf(**params), tcfm(chan,pcode,...) fixed-frame test TX. Each formats a CLI line and sends it; capture the echoed resolved-param block where useful (see cli-protocol.md §3/§5). Red/green TDD.
acceptance_criteria: New Device methods unit-tested via tests/test_device.py ScriptedSerial; param dicts serialize to correct CLI strings; resolved-param echo parsed; existing suite still green.

title: Transponder: iOS sub-view
description: iOS Transponder sub-screen: start/stop via opcode; show 'landmark active' + answered-poll count from notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Compiles; start/stop writes transponder opcode; status renders.

title: Beacon: BeaconController (TCFM fixed-frame beacon, stock fw)
description: uwb_explorer/experiments/beacon.py BeaconController: periodic fixed-frame TX using stock-fw TCFM (Device.tcfm) with configurable channel/pcode/interval. This is the no-new-firmware beacon; arbitrary payload is the separate firmware bead. Red/green TDD with ScriptedSerial.
acceptance_criteria: Controller emits correct TCFM CLI for given chan/pcode/interval; start/stop tested; existing suite green.

title: Beacon: custom-firmware arbitrary-payload TX
description: firmware/ble/: add an arbitrary-payload beacon TX (dwt_writetxdata/dwt_starttx) triggered by a new control opcode (F2 family) carrying the payload bytes; build via firmware/build-ble.sh. TDD the FRAME BUILDER in tests/c/ (see test_framefmt/test_framepoll patterns); the radio TX itself is on-board verified (not unit-testable). Note half-duplex: pause the listener while beaconing.
acceptance_criteria: tests/c frame-builder tests pass (red/green); opcode parsed in firmware; beacon frame observed on a second board / LISTENER capture; documented in EXPERIMENTS.md.

title: Beacon: web view
description: Web Experiments hub Beacon panel: set channel/pcode/interval (+payload once B2 lands), start/stop, show TX-active state. TDD with FakeDev.
acceptance_criteria: Start/stop + params wired to BeaconController; TX-active state renders in tests.

title: Beacon: iOS sub-view
description: iOS Beacon sub-screen: params + payload field, start/stop via opcode, TX-active indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Compiles; start/stop writes beacon opcode with params; indicator reflects state.

title: Fuzzer: custom-firmware malformed-frame TX primitive
description: firmware/ble/: TX primitive that emits deliberately malformed 802.15.4z frames (bad CRC, reserved/invalid frame type, oversized PHR, truncated MAC header, illegal STS config) selectable by a control opcode + case id. Build via build-ble.sh. TDD the malformed-frame BUILDERS in tests/c/; radio TX on-board verified. SECURITY/ETHICS: own devices or authorized targets only; document scope in EXPERIMENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria: tests/c builder tests per fuzz case (red/green); opcode+case-id parsed; a chosen malformed frame observed via LISTENER capture; ethics/scope note in docs.

title: Fuzzer: FuzzerController (case catalog + capture reactions)
description: uwb_explorer/experiments/fuzzer.py FuzzerController: catalog of fuzz cases, trigger a case (opcode -> firmware Z1), then capture nearby-device reactions by switching to LISTENER and logging frames/PHY events with structured timestamps. Red/green TDD the case catalog + reaction-logging against ScriptedSerial (real TX needs Z1).
acceptance_criteria: Case catalog enumerated + tested; scripted post-fuzz LISTENER frames captured into a structured log; safe stop restores IDLE.

title: Fuzzer: web view
description: Web Experiments hub Fuzzer panel: pick a fuzz case, fire it, show captured reactions log. Include an on-screen 'authorized targets only' note. TDD with FakeDev.
acceptance_criteria: Case picker + fire wired to FuzzerController; reaction log renders in tests; ethics note visible.

title: Fuzzer: iOS sub-view
description: iOS Fuzzer sub-screen: case picker, fire via opcode, reactions list; 'authorized targets only' banner.
acceptance_criteria: Compiles; fire writes fuzzer opcode+case; reactions render; banner shown.

title: Harden tcfm signature + CLI-value validation + dispatcher action guard
description:
Follow-ups surfaced by the 1hu.1/1hu.2 validators (non-blocking, but fix before consumers rely on them):
1. Device.tcfm(chan,pcode,count,interval) accepts chan/pcode but ignores them — dev.tcfm(chan=5,pcode=9) silently sends bare 'tcfm', dropping caller intent. Fix: either drop chan/pcode from the signature, or wire them via set_uwbcfg before tcfm, or raise. (device.py ~127)
2. Partial tcfm: tcfm(count=100) with interval omitted silently falls through to bare 'tcfm'. Send 'tcfm <count>' if firmware allows a single positional, or raise on partial. Add tests either way. (device.py ~128)
3. No validation on interpolated values in _flags()/set_uwbcfg — a value containing a space/newline would break or inject the CLI line. Add light validation (reject whitespace/control chars). Low risk today (callers pass ints).
4. Dispatcher.dispatch trusts cmd.action; a hand-built ExperimentCommand with an arbitrary action string lets getattr reach any attribute (non-callable -> TypeError, dunders reachable). Guard: check cmd.action in ACTIONS.values() at top of dispatch, else ValueError. (experiments/control.py)
5. Document duplicate-arg-key last-wins behavior in docs/EXPERIMENTS.md (chan=9,chan=10 -> chan=10).
Red/green TDD each fix.
acceptance_criteria: tcfm no longer silently drops params (tests cover chan/pcode + partial forms); _flags/set_uwbcfg reject whitespace/control chars in values; Dispatcher rejects invalid action verbs; EXPERIMENTS.md notes duplicate-key behavior; full suite green.

comments.author: ben
comments.text: From 1hu.3 validation (web endpoints): also (a) wrap DashboardServer's dispatch() call in try/except -> clean 5xx JSON, so a real controller raising (serial wedge / unregistered letter) doesn't surface as a 500 traceback — matters once real controllers land in .5/.8/.11/.16; (b) fold the Content-Length int() into the try block in web.py do_POST so a non-numeric header returns 400 not 500.

comments.author: ben
comments.text: From 1hu.5 validation (scanner): ScannerController._parse_csv does int(tok) with no guard — a non-integer arg (e.g. pcodes='abc' arriving from the web/iOS scanner view or dispatcher) raises ValueError and crashes start(). Validate args and return a clean error instead. Also minor: ScanResults rssi field is initialized but never populated (wire from RangeEntry or drop); and consider having set_uwbcfg drain its own 'ok' ack so the scanner's phantom-avoidance isn't a positional flush.

comments.author: ben
comments.text: From 1hu.6 validation: _LazyDispatcher.set_device is called from board_loop's thread while HTTP threads read it — benign scalar-ref race under GIL today, but fold into the same try/except-wrap + a note/lock when the live half-duplex board arbitration (listener loop vs active experiment sharing the one serial port) is built.

title: Experiment control protocol + Pi dispatcher + docs/EXPERIMENTS.md
description: Define ONE shared experiment control convention documented like blecodec KEY_MAP. Extend the existing 6e5f control-characteristic command set (today F1/F0/S<sts>) with an experiment opcode family (e.g. 'X<exp><action> <args>'). Add a Pi-side dispatcher mapping a control command -> experiment controller start/stop/status. Write docs/EXPERIMENTS.md with the opcode table so firmware/Pi/iOS stay in agreement. Red/green TDD the parse/format + dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: docs/EXPERIMENTS.md opcode table; tested parse/format that round-trips; dispatcher routes opcodes to controller stubs and rejects unknown opcodes.

title: Web Experiments hub: generic start/stop/status endpoints
description: Add an Experiments section/route to uwb_explorer/web.py DashboardServer with generic POST start/stop + GET status endpoints that call the F2 dispatcher. Wire serve.py so the single board_loop pauses and hands the port to the running experiment (half-duplex), then resumes sniffing on stop. Red/green TDD with tests/test_web.py FakeDev.
acceptance_criteria: POST start/stop + status endpoints tested with FakeDev; status reflects the running experiment; sniffer resumes after stop; no port contention.

title: iOS Experiments hub view + BLEManager command sender
description: Add ExperimentsView.swift (NavigationStack list -> per-experiment sub-screens) to the real target ios/UWBExplorer/UWBExplorer/UWBExplorer/, reachable from ContentView. Add sendExperimentCommand(opcode,args) to BLEManager reusing the existing writeValue control-characteristic path (BLEManager.swift ~L93/256-262). Placeholder sub-screens are fine; experiments fill them. Add a UI test stub per the existing UITests target.
acceptance_criteria: Hub compiles and lists the 4 experiments with navigation to placeholder sub-views; command sender writes a well-formed opcode to the 6e5f control characteristic; builds clean.

title: Scanner: ScannerController (INITF sweep + responder discovery)
description: uwb_explorer/experiments/scanner.py ScannerController: sweep channels 5/9 x preamble codes 9-12 x PRF sets driving Device.start_initf(...) per combo, listen a short window for responder replies / RX events via Device.poll_events()+parser, aggregate into a discovered-devices model (which combo replied, RSSI, first/last seen). Reuse the sweep approach in tools/detect.py. Red/green TDD the sweep-plan generator + aggregation with ScriptedSerial — no hardware.
acceptance_criteria: Sweep-plan generator tested; scripted responder replies aggregate into a discovered list; unknown/empty windows handled; clean stop restores IDLE.

title: Scanner: web view (live discovered-devices table)
description: Web Experiments hub Scanner panel: start/stop the sweep via the F3 endpoints, render a live table of discovered PHY configs (channel/pcode/PRF, RSSI, last seen) and current sweep position. TDD render/state with FakeDev.
acceptance_criteria: Start/stop wired to ScannerController; table updates from scripted discoveries in tests; sweep progress shown.

title: Scanner: iOS sub-view
description: iOS Scanner sub-screen under ExperimentsView: Start/Stop sends the scanner opcode via BLEManager.sendExperimentCommand; list discovered devices from notifications. Match existing SwiftUI style (see HistoryView/LearnView).
acceptance_criteria: Sub-view compiles; start/stop writes the scanner opcode; discovered list renders from a decoded notification model.

title: Transponder: TransponderController (permissive RESPF)
description: uwb_explorer/experiments/transponder.py TransponderController: run Device.start_respf(...) across a configurable set of PHY combos so the board answers polls from unknown initiators (a discoverable UWB landmark). Report answered polls / initiator addrs / RSSI. Red/green TDD with ScriptedSerial.
acceptance_criteria: Controller cycles configs; scripted incoming polls -> answered-poll report tested; clean stop.

title: Transponder: web view
description: Web Experiments hub Transponder panel: start/stop, show config being advertised + a log of answered polls. TDD with FakeDev.
acceptance_criteria: Start/stop wired to TransponderController; answered-poll log renders in tests.
